calendar.matters:
Petition To 1: Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment; 2) Preclude The Use Of Trust Funds To Defend Trust Contest
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Off Calendar

Petition For Determination Of Validity Of Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement; Declaration Of Kevin P. Obrien In Support
Off Calendar Jan-13-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey R. Liu for petitioner and former trust beneficiary Albert Martin, who was also present.
Kevin P. O'Brien for respondent, petitioner and trustee Robert Gordon, who was also present.
Gregory Woodbury, objector in pro per.

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Amendment and to Preclude Use of Trust Funds to Defend Trust Contest; 2) Petition for Determination of Validity of Trust Provisions; 3) Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement; and 4) Order of Status Hearing.

Mr. O'Brien addressed the Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement and argued that the proposed settlement was reasonable and in the best interests of the trust and its beneficiaries. He stated that notice of mediation had been provided pursuant to the Court's prior Breslin order and that the settlement reflected the terms necessary to resolve the litigation.

Mr. Woodbury objected to approval of the settlement, asserting that he did not receive notice of the mediation while he was overseas and that the settlement unfairly reduced his beneficial interest in the trust. Mr. Woodbury stated that he would have participated in the mediation had he been aware of it and argued that the allocation of settlement funding was inequitable.

The Court questioned counsel regarding notice of mediation and the basis for the settlement allocation. The Court noted that notice had been provided to Mr. Woodbury at the address of record and that no request for special notice or alternative service had been filed.

After hearing argument, the Court took the Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement under submission with the intent to grant.

The Court took the Order of Status Hearing off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

-----------------------------------------------------------------
Post-script:
Following the hearing, the Court continued the petitions to determine validity of trust provisions to March 30, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Off Calendar

Petition To 1: Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment; 2) Preclude The Use Of Trust Funds To Defend Trust Contest
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Continued To Mar-30-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Validity Of Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Continued To Mar-30-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Oct-13-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Oct-13-2025 Continued To Jan-12-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To 1: Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment; 2) Preclude The Use Of Trust Funds To Defend Trust Contest
Off Calendar Oct-13-2025 Continued To Jan-12-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Validity Of Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Oct-13-2025 Continued To Jan-12-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Validity Of Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Jun-16-2025 Continued To Oct-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Nagakawa for Albert John Martin, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Kevin P. O'Brien for and with Robert Gordon, Respondent/Trustee (via Zoom).

Mr. O'Brien informed the Court that the parties have exchanged written discovery and are now prepared to proceed to private mediation. Counsel requested that the Court issue a Breslin order to ensure notice is provided to all interested persons and to address concerns of participation by multiple beneficiaries.

The Court agreed and confirmed it would issue a Breslin order directing the parties to complete mediation on or before September 30, 2025. The parties are ordered to file a joint status report on mediation by October 6, 2025. The Court set a hearing for October 13, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, on status of mediation. The underlying petitions were continued to be heard on the same date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To 1: Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment; 2) Preclude The Use Of Trust Funds To Defend Trust Contest
Off Calendar Jun-16-2025 Continued To Oct-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Validity Of Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Mar-17-2025 Continued To Jun-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey R. Liu for Albert John Martin, Petitioner/Former Trust Beneficiary (via Zoom).
Kevin P. O'Brien for and with Robert Gordon, Respondent/Petitioner/Trustee (via Zoom).

The court acknowledged the filing of Mr. Martin's response to the trustee's petition, which had been electronically filed on March 12, 2025. Mr. O'Brien stated that he had not yet reviewed the filing. Both Mr. Liu and Mr. O'Brien agreed that mediation may be appropriate, but it was noted that further discovery is still pending.

The court continued the matter to June 16, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow for discovery.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To 1: Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment; 2) Preclude The Use Of Trust Funds To Defend Trust Contest
Off Calendar Mar-17-2025 Continued To Jun-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To 1: Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment; 2) Preclude The Use Of Trust Funds To Defend Trust Contest
Off Calendar Jan-15-2025 Continued To Mar-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Validity Of Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Jan-15-2025 Continued To Mar-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey Z. Liu for Albert John Martin, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Kevin P. O' Brien for and with Robert Gordon, Respondent/Trustee (via Zoom).

The court acknowledged that there were competing petitions in this matter and sought clarification on procedural issues. Mr. Liu requested additional time to respond to Robert Gordon's petition. Mr. O'Brien agreed to a continuance and proposed a timeline of 90 days. The court expressed a preference for a shorter continuance and suggested March 17, 2025. Both sides agreed to the proposed date.

The court continued the matter to March 17, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
